text:
For developers running two or more coding agents in parallel on one repo — Claude Code next to Codex next to OpenCode — who spend their day hand-carrying context, decisions, and "hey, the other one changed that file" between terminals, **tenex-edge is an identity and awareness fabric for the agents you already run**: each agent gets a durable, self-owned identity, broadcasts a live one-line "what I'm doing," and can @mention any other agent so the message lands in that agent's terminal as a real conversational turn — across hosts. Unlike orchestrators and worktree-isolation tools (which spawn, own, or wall off agents that still can't see each other), and unlike Claude Code's Agent Teams (Claude-only, gone when the session ends), tenex-edge connects agents it didn't build and gives each one an identity that survives sessions, restarts, and machines. Nobody else has that combination; that's the whitespace, verified.

Category label to use in copy: **"an identity and awareness fabric for coding agents."** Never "orchestrator," never "agent OS," never "swarm."
 [...] **USP / whitespace (verified uncontested in a ~90-tool field):** durable, self-owned, cryptographic identity for coding agents that is **host-neutral AND cross-session AND cross-machine at once**. Every rival is ephemeral session IDs or locally-registered names — hcom, mcp_agent_mail, and Claude Code's own Agent Teams included.

**Category label:** "an identity and awareness fabric for coding agents." Never *orchestration / swarm / agent OS* — all saturated.
